title: pow: h16 replay set maps 16-byte key to 32-byte hash; wasted space
description: The PoW replay set (state.inner.pow_replay_set) keys on h16 ([u8;16]) and stores an Instant. The h16 is already the first 16 bytes of the double-SHA256 — it uniquely identifies the token. No issue there. However, the map value is Instant (platform size, typically 16 bytes on Linux). Total per-entry cost is 32 bytes key (DashMap alignment) + 16 bytes value + overhead. At 1M cap that's ~48MB. The retention TTL is 600s matching PoW staleness window, which is correct. This is fine as-is; documenting the sizing in a comment near MAX_REPLAY_ENTRIES would prevent future over-estimation of memory cost.
